slides.title: Using outbox-md
slides.subtitle: From zero to your first AI-applied edit

slides.title: 1 · Run it
bullets: Every .md in the folder becomes a document

slides.title: 2 · Comment
bullets: Open a document and highlight any text
It appears in the Outbox panel
The file itself is untouched — it just waits in the queue

slides.title: 3 · Connect an agent
bullets: Point any MCP client at http://localhost:8080/mcp
Any agent works — no keys stored by the app

slides.title: 4 · Accept
bullets: The .md file is rewritten and versioned
Other comments re-anchor to follow their text

slides.title: That's the loop
bullets: Repeat to iterate a rough draft into a finished spec

lines.speaker: Andrew
lines.text: Alright Ava, show me how to actually use outbox-md.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Sure. It's four steps: run it, comment, connect an agent, and accept. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: First, run the container and point it at a folder of Markdown files, then open localhost port eight thousand eighty in your browser. Every Markdown file in that folder becomes a document you can review.

lines.speaker: Andrew
lines.text: So it just reads my existing specs folder — nothing to upload.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Right. Now open a document, highlight any text, and click Comment on selection. Your comment shows up in the Outbox panel, and the file itself stays untouched — the comment just waits in the queue.

lines.speaker: Andrew
lines.text: And the AI — how does it pick up the work?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You point any MCP client at localhost slash m-c-p. It can list the open comments, claim one, and propose an edit. The app stores no keys — you bring your own agent.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: So the flow is simple: you comment, the agent claims and proposes, and you decide.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: When you're happy, click Accept. The Markdown file is rewritten and versioned, and every other comment re-anchors to follow its text.

lines.speaker: Andrew
lines.text: So the file on disk is always the latest approved version.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Always — ordered, safe, and fully auditable. Repeat that loop to turn a rough AI draft into a finished spec.

lines.speaker: Andrew
lines.text: Great. And it's open for contributions?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: It is. Come build with us — the link's right below.
